import collections

def can_construct_ransom_note(note_text: str, magazine_text: str) -> bool:
    """
    Checks if a ransom note can be constructed from the words in a magazine.

    Args:
        note_text: The text of the ransom note.
        magazine_text: The text available in the magazine.

    Returns:
        True if the note can be constructed, False otherwise.
    """
    magazine_words = [word.lower() for word in magazine_text.split()]
    note_words = [word.lower() for word in note_text.split()]

    magazine_word_counts = collections.Counter(magazine_words)
    note_word_counts = collections.Counter(note_words)

    for word, required_count in note_word_counts.items():
        if magazine_word_counts[word] < required_count:
            return False
            
    return True

if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Test Cases
    print(can_construct_ransom_note("give me one grand today night", "give one grand today night")) # True
    print(can_construct_ransom_note("two times three is not four", "two times two is four"))      # False (missing 'three', 'not')
    print(can_construct_ransom_note("apple banana", "apple orange banana"))                     # True
    print(can_construct_ransom_note("hello world", "world hello"))                              # True
    print(can_construct_ransom_note("hello hello", "hello world"))                              # False (not enough 'hello')
    print(can_construct_ransom_note("", "any text here"))                                       # True (empty note is always constructible)
    print(can_construct_ransom_note("test", ""))                                                # False
    print(can_construct_ransom_note("this is a test", "this is only a test but not this"))     # True
    print(can_construct_ransom_note("THIS", "this"))                                            # True (case-insensitive)
